mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 65% of months where inflation exceeds the 2.5% threshold over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal shows a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP growth during a contractionary phase signal deepening economic weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during contraction periods with statistical significance over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of industrial production directly correlate with increased corporate revenue generation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across all market regimes over a 3-year rolling test.
